metadata.name: fluentogram
metadata.summary: A proper way to use an i18n mechanism with Aiogram3.

modules.name: fluentogram.exceptions.root_locale_translator
documentation: Custom exception, raises when main, core translator does not exist

classes.name: fluentogram.exceptions.root_locale_translator.NotImplementedRootLocaleTranslator
documentation: This exception is raised when TranslatorHub has no translator for root locale and being impossible to work.

methods.name: fluentogram.src.impl.translator.FluentTranslator.get
documentation:
STR100: Calling format with insecure string.
Route questions to --> https://github.com/django-ftl/fluent-compiler
Single-locale Translator, implemented with fluent_compiler Bundles

methods.name: fluentogram.src.impl.filter.FText.__init__

classes.name: fluentogram.src.impl.runner.TranslatorRunner

methods.name: fluentogram.src.impl.runner.TranslatorRunner.get
documentation: Fastest, direct way to use translator, without sugar-like typing supported attribute access way
This is one-shot per Telegram event translator with attrib tracer access way.

classes.name: fluentogram.src.impl.attrib_tracer.AttribTracer

methods.name: fluentogram.src.impl.attrib_tracer.AttribTracer.__getattr__
documentation: This method exists to map the "obj.attrib1.attrib2" access to "attrib1-attrib2" key.
Attribute tracer class for obj.attrib1.attrib2 access

classes.name: fluentogram.src.impl.transformers.datetime_transformer.DateTimeTransformer
documentation:
This transformer converts a default python datetime object to FluentDate
Typings refer to https://github.com/tc39/ecma402

classes.name: fluentogram.src.impl.transformers.money_transformer.MoneyTransformer
documentation:
This transformer converts a decimal object to FluentNumber with proper metadata.
Typings refer to https://github.com/tc39/ecma402

modules.name: fluentogram.src.abc.transformer
documentation: An AbstractDataTransformer object, using to transform any data before being passed to translator directly.

classes.name: fluentogram.src.abc.transformer.AbstractDataTransformer

methods.name: fluentogram.src.abc.transformer.AbstractDataTransformer.__new__
documentation:
Using incoming data, create an object representation of these data for your translator via all needed
parameters using kwargs

These transformers inspired by Functions of Project Fluent by Mozilla.
Of course, it's a simple function, like a

def function(money: Union[int, float], **kwargs) -> str: ...

which result passes through translator, into engine itself, like a Fluent or anything else.

classes.name: fluentogram.src.abc.translator.AbstractTranslator

methods.name: fluentogram.src.abc.translator.AbstractTranslator.get
documentation:
Convert a translation key to a translated text string.
Use kwargs dict to pass external data to the translator.
Expects to be fast and furious.
A translator class, implements key-value interface for your translator mechanism.

modules.name: fluentogram.src.impl.transator_hubs.translator_hub
documentation: A Translator Hub, using as factory for Translator objects

classes.name: fluentogram.src.impl.transator_hubs.translator_hub.TranslatorHub

methods.name: fluentogram.src.impl.transator_hubs.translator_hub.TranslatorHub.get_translator_by_locale
documentation:
Here is a little tricky moment.
There should be like a one-shot scheme.
For proper isolation, function returns TranslatorRunner new instance every time, not the same translator.
This trick makes "obj.attribute1.attribute2" access to be able.
You are able to do what you want, refer to the abstract class.
This class implements a storage for all single-locale translators.

modules.name: fluentogram.src.abc.translator_hub
documentation: An abstract base for the Translator Hub and Key/Value Translator Hub objects

classes.name: fluentogram.src.abc.translator_hub.AbstractTranslatorsHub

methods.name: fluentogram.src.abc.translator_hub.AbstractTranslatorsHub.get_translator_by_locale
documentation: Returns a Translator object by selected locale
This class should contain a couple of translator objects, usually one object per one locale.

methods.name: fluentogram.src.abc.translator_hub.AbstractKvTranslatorHub.from_storage
documentation: Initializes the Translator Hub with the provided storage

classes.name: fluentogram.src.abc.misc.AbstractAttribTracer

methods.name: fluentogram.src.abc.misc.AbstractAttribTracer.__getattr__
documentation: This method exists to map the "obj.attrib1.attrib2" access to "attrib1-attrib2" key.

Implements a mechanism for tracing attributes access way.

Like a pretty simple, external-typing supported version of the translator.get("some-key-for-translation")

Equivalent to obj.some.key.for.translation(**some_kwargs)
